title: Cómo levanto un proyecto de Bancard dockerizado?

_links.self:
[PRIVATE_URL_WITH_CREDENTIALS] the project is downloaded, open a terminal in the root directory of the project and execute the command:

This script will be responsible for copying the configuration files, creating the databases, running the migrations, and spinning up a Docker container with the service and another one with Sidekiq, along with all the necessary containers for the proper functioning of the service, such as a container with MySQL and another one with Redis.

  • Check the service documentation to confirm that the service is running in the following URL:
    http://localhost:8079/payment-link/doc/0.1/

  • Once the service is running, access a terminal within the service container with the following command:

  • Once inside the container, run the tests to confirm that the service is properly configured and ready for development.

    • MISSION ACCOMPLISHED!

    Project without docker-compose

    For this document, the billing-service project will be used as an example. However, please note that the configuration is the same for all services without docker-compose.

    1. Run two Docker containers, one with MySQL and another with Redis, using the following commands:

      Note: For the case of the portals, simply start the Redis container.

    2. Clone the project using SSH.

    3. Copy all files/folders that contain .sample in their name to their equivalent without the .sample.
      Example for a file and a folder:

      Note: The .sample files are versioned in the repository.

    4. Configure the database.yml file to use the database container (develop and testing).
      MAC: host: docker.for.mac.host.internal
      Linux: host: 127.0.0.1
      Note: This step does not apply to the portals.

    5. Configure the services.yml file to use the staging services.
      To do this, search for the services.yml file in bancard-configurations for the respective project and modify the URL with the service’s proxy IP (10.100.14.73).

      Example for billing-service:
      > In this case you will need to use the file bancard-configurations/bancard-billing-service/staging/common/services.yml
      > For auth-service, change services.auth.staging to 10.100.14.73
      > Do the same for all the services

    6. Configure Redis in services_configuration.yml by modifying the 'host' key:
      MAC: host: docker.for.mac.host.internal
      Linux: host: 127.0.0.1

      The redis configuration in MAC should look like this:

      For the case of the portals, the configuration file is called application_configuration.yml.

    7. Create databases:
      Execute the terminal.sh script. This will position us inside the project container. Once there, run the following commands to create the development and test databases:

      Note: This step does not apply to the portals.

    8. Start the Docker container for the project by executing the run.sh file:
